tasks.title: Create the Kotlin Multiplatform Android project foundation
tasks.summary: Create a buildable Kotlin Multiplatform and Compose Multiplatform project with Android as the only shipped MVP target and clear shared/platform module boundaries.
tasks.objective: Establish the smallest reproducible application skeleton that later tasks can extend without changing the accepted architecture.
tasks.context: The repository currently contains lifecycle artifacts but no application implementation. The accepted plan assigns shared domain/UI code and Android platform adapters to separate areas.
outputs: Buildable shared and Android application modules
Minimal Android launcher and Compose root
Developer setup documentation and baseline CI-ready commands
implementation_notes: Configure Android minimum SDK to match Android 10/API 29 unless a documented toolchain constraint requires a compatible equivalent.
Do not add iOS or desktop deliverables; shared code should avoid unnecessary Android coupling.
Keep dependency versions centralized and do not add network, auth, analytics, or cloud SDKs.
acceptance_criteria: A clean checkout can invoke the documented Gradle wrapper commands without manual file edits.
The Android debug application assembles and launches to a minimal Compose root.
Shared domain code compiles without importing Android UI, filesystem, database, or ONNX APIs.
Static checks and unit-test source sets are wired for later tasks.
verification: Run the Gradle wrapper version command.
Run the Android debug assemble task.
Run configured shared unit tests and static checks.
Inspect the dependency graph for unapproved network or telemetry libraries.
edge_cases: Offline Gradle execution after dependencies are cached
tasks.handoff_notes: All application tasks use the documented wrapper commands and extend these module boundaries.

tasks.title: Implement local album persistence behind the shared repository
tasks.summary: Implement transactional Android persistence for album metadata and private app-managed image references behind AlbumRepository.
tasks.objective: Provide restart-safe local album storage without leaking database or Android types into shared domain code.
tasks.context: The MVP is local-only and album entries must survive restarts while supporting edits, deletes, search, and favorite filtering.
outputs: Persistence unit and Android integration tests
implementation_notes: Store only managed relative image identifiers or validated private paths, never transient gallery URIs as durable references.
Define deterministic search behavior and sort order.
Keep database transactions explicit for multi-record operations.
acceptance_criteria: Create, observe, update, favorite, annotate, search/filter, and delete operations satisfy the shared contract.
Entries survive process recreation and application restart.
Database details do not escape the adapter boundary.
Deleting an entry has a documented and tested managed-image cleanup policy.
verification: Run repository contract tests against the real adapter.
Inspect stored records for absence of original external URIs and unnecessary metadata.

tasks.title: Implement Android image acquisition and sanitization adapters
tasks.summary: Implement camera and system gallery acquisition, deterministic orientation handling, preprocessing-source preparation, metadata removal, and private managed-image storage.
tasks.objective: Turn user-selected images into stable private inputs without retaining unnecessary location or device metadata.
tasks.context: Both camera and gallery inputs are required, but only app-managed sanitized copies may back durable album entries.
outputs: Private managed-image store and cleanup behavior
implementation_notes: Prefer system picker/camera contracts that minimize broad storage permissions.
Decode with bounded memory and preserve correct visual orientation before stripping metadata.
Use atomic file creation/rename where possible and clean temporary files on cancel or error.
acceptance_criteria: Camera and gallery flows each produce a readable private app-managed image.
The managed copy renders with correct orientation and contains no unnecessary EXIF location/device metadata.
Canceled, malformed, oversized, and unreadable inputs return recoverable errors without orphaned durable entries.
The app does not rely on permanent access to the original gallery item.
verification: Run instrumentation tests with rotated, metadata-rich, large, malformed, and canceled fixtures.
Inspect managed outputs with an independent metadata reader.
Measure peak decode memory on representative large images.

tasks.title: Implement recognition orchestration with the fake engine
tasks.summary: Implement shared recognition use cases and presentation state against RecognitionEngine using the deterministic fake adapter until ONNX integration is ready.
tasks.objective: Unblock end-to-end application flow while preserving one replaceable inference boundary.
tasks.context: The accepted planning package explicitly permits a fake RecognitionEngine, and real inference belongs to the ML lane.
outputs: Unit tests driven by fake responses
implementation_notes: Run recognition away from the UI thread and make concurrent submissions deterministic.
Preserve candidate order, scores, model version, and timing without inventing confidence claims.
Manual correction is album metadata only and never a training submission.
acceptance_criteria: Every contract result state maps to an explicit presentation state.
Duplicate taps, cancellation, retry, and stale completion cannot save the wrong result.
A candidate confirmation or manual correction produces a valid save command but does not persist until explicitly confirmed.
Replacing the fake engine with the production adapter requires no screen or domain contract change.
verification: Run deterministic unit tests for success, uncertain, unsupported, error, cancellation, retry, and out-of-order completion.
Run concurrency tests with controlled fake delays.

tasks.title: Implement validated album export and atomic import
tasks.summary: Implement versioned, checksummed archive export and two-phase validate-then-commit import behind shared use cases.
tasks.objective: Provide reliable manual backup and transfer without partial mutation or internal database coupling.
tasks.context: The accepted MVP requires manual export/import, and the archive contract defines compatibility, conflict, privacy, and path-safety rules.
implementation_notes: Validate archive version, paths, declared sizes, checksums, entry references, and resource limits before database/file mutation.
Stage imported files privately and commit metadata/files as one recoverable operation.
Return a human-readable preview and structured errors for UI consumption.
acceptance_criteria: A valid export imports into an empty profile with equivalent album data and image bytes.
Corrupt, incompatible, traversal, missing-file, duplicate, oversized, or partial archives cause no partial durable mutation.
Conflict behavior is deterministic and matches the accepted contract.
Exports contain sanitized managed copies and no internal absolute paths.
verification: Run every positive and negative contract fixture.
Inject failures during staging and commit and verify rollback/cleanup.
Inspect archive contents for path and metadata privacy.
edge_cases: Zip bomb or excessive file count
tasks.handoff_notes: The application-experience batch can bind Data Transfer UI directly to the structured preview and result types.
